an11.502-981:0.4: Untitled Discourses on the Eye, Etc.
an11.502-981:1.1:
1
"Bhikkhus, possessing eleven factors, a cowherd is capable of keeping and rearing a herd of cattle.
What eleven? Here, (1) a cowherd has knowledge of form; (2) he is skilled in characteristics; (3) he picks out flies’ eggs; (4) he dresses wounds; (5) he smokes out the sheds; (6) he knows the watering place; (7) he knows what it is to have drunk; (8) he knows the road; (9) he is skilled in pastures; (10) he does not milk dry; and (11) he shows extra veneration to those bulls who are fathers and leaders of the herd. Possessing these eleven factors, a cowherd is capable of keeping and rearing a herd of cattle.
an11.502-981:2.2:
2
"So too, bhikkhus, possessing eleven qualities, a bhikkhu is capable of dwelling contemplating impermanence in the eye …
… is capable of dwelling contemplating relinquishment in examination of phenomena … "
